Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] {{DEFAULTSORT:さひめやましんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)}}[[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:島根県の旧村社]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:島根県の神社]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8280005005208 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '島根県益田市乙子町51', 'language': 'ja'} == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Hirefuri|lt=Mount Hirefuri|ceb|Hirefuri-yama|WD=Q31703210|1=Mount Hirefuri}} [[JA:佐毘売山神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11383230}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=September 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Sahimeyama Shrine | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=Masuda}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}} Otsugo-chō 51 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto
Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) / former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Shimane|label=Sahimeyama
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Sahimeyama Shrine''' (さひめやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Masuda|Q753731|WD=Q753731|1=Masuda, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Shimane Prefecture|Q132751|WD=Q132751|1=Shimane Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. {{ill|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine (Ōda)|Q11383231|WD=Q11383231|lt=Sahimeyama Shrine|1=Sahimeyama Shrine}} at {{ill|lt=Iwami Ginzan|Q174139|WD=Q174139|1=Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine}} ({{ill|lt=Ōda|Q384796|WD=Q384796|1=Ōda, Shimane}}) is a branch shrine of this shrine. === Enshrined deities === Its principal deities are {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto and Kanayamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}. Enshrined in the paired hall are {{ill|lt=Haniyasuhime|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}}, {{ill|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōyamatsumi}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyahime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahime-no-Mikoto|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}}; in a separate hall are {{ill|lt=Okami-no-Kami|Q644201|WD=Q644201|lt=Kurakura-no-Kami|1=Kuraokami}} and Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ankan|Q241765|WD=Q241765|1=Emperor Ankan}}). === History === The origin of its foundation is unknown. At first it stood on the summit of Hirefuri-yama (Sahimeyama), enshrining three goddesses - Kanayamahime, Haniyamahime, and Konohanasakuyahime - and was called “Himeyama Shrine.” “Sahimeyama” is “Himeyama” with the prefix “sa.” In {{ill|lt=Kanpyō|Q1346292|WD=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 5 ({{ill|lt=893|Q24762|WD=Q24762|1=893}}), Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto, a deity of mines, was invited from {{ill|lt=Nangū Taisha|Q704967|WD=Q704967|1=Nangū Taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and mountain deity Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto was also enshrined, so it was also called “Gosha Daigongen.” It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as “Sahimeyama Shrine, {{ill|lt=Mino District|Q2341285|WD=Q2341285|1=Mino District, Shimane}}, {{ill|lt=Iwami Province|Q1053709|WD=Q1053709|1=Iwami Province}},” and ranked as a small shrine. In {{ill|lt=Jōji|Q1146153|WD=Q1146153|1=Jōji}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1365|Q6190|WD=Q6190|1=1365}}), a drought struck the area; after prayers for rain on Hirefuri-yama, rain fell, and water deity Kurakura-no-Kami and {{ill|lt=Zaō Gongen|Q10514059|WD=Q10514059|1=Zaō Gongen}} were merged in from {{ill|lt=Yoshino-yama|Q1036938|WD=Q1036938|1=Mount Yoshino}} in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, after which it also came to be called “Zaō Daigongen.” In {{ill|lt=Bun'an|Q1193586|WD=Q1193586|1=Bun'an}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1446|Q6430|WD=Q6430|1=1446}}), it moved from the summit to the mountainside, and later in {{ill|lt=Manji|Q1145295|WD=Q1145295|1=Manji (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}}), it moved again to the present site. In Kansei 3 ({{ill|lt=1791|Q7784|WD=Q7784|1=1791}}), it was renamed to the current shrine name recorded in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. Due to the Meiji-era {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, Zaō Gongen as an enshrined deity was changed to Hirokuni Oshitake Kanahi Tennō, regarded as Zaō Gongen’s Buddhist original. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture|Q11476446|WD=Q11476446|1=Tourist attractions in Shimane Prefecture}} *{{ill|lt=Otsugo Sayahime|Q24863501|WD=Q24863501|1=Otsugo Sayahime}} === External links === *[http://www.sahimeyama-jinja.jp/index.html Sahimeyama Shrine homepage] == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sahimeyama Shrine (Q11383230)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Iwami Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:石見国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:益田市の建築物]] [[Category:益田市の歴史]]
